sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The view has scrolled. Now I can see:
- 2 green N blocks at the top
- 3 purple blocks with yellow centers in the middle
- Some green N blocks on the left and right
- The player is on the right side
- 3 purple blocks at the bottom
The N blocks seem to have been removed from the middle area but there are still some remaining. The layout has changed significantly. Let me check the current state and continue removing N blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current N blocks
seg = current_frame.segmentation
n_blocks = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks: {len(n_blocks)}")
for n in n_blocks:
    center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    print(f"  N at ({center_r}, {center_c})")

# Click them one at a time
for n in n_blocks:
    center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': center_r, 'col': center_c}])
    if last_action_result.get('game_over') or last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print(f"Stopped: game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}, level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}")
        break

seg2 = current_frame.segmentation
n_remaining = [n for n in seg2['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"N blocks remaining: {len(n_remaining)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
